title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission · Behavior Case: Pathlib Semantics
summary: Recursive discovery follows a symlink outside content, includes hidden drafts and generated output, treats directories as documents, and overwrites two sources onto one route. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pathlib semantics increment in bounded content-discovery manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.
Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-pathlib-semantics — Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-paths-content-discovery-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-paths-content-discovery-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_paths_content_discovery_5(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"

    evidence_axes_2kng08 = (
        "reader2kng08",
        "source7129",
        "parse80gnk2",
        "route2kng087129",
        "artifact71292kng08",

    if root_escapes > 0:

    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"

options.text: Keep the Pathlib Semantics artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the pathlib semantics defect evidence boundary for Pathlib Semantics.

For repair the pathlib semantics defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Pathlib Semantics for bounded content-discovery manifest.
Reconstruct the conditions for Pathlib Semantics, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pathlib Semantics model · Theory Pathlib Semantics
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Pathlib Semantics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5594 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a bounded content manifest with explicit rejection, root, and collision evidence; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pathlib Semantics model · Theory Pathlib Semantics.
steps.why: Retrieving Pathlib Semantics through the concrete bounded content-discovery manifest case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pathlib Semantics model · Theory Pathlib Semantics, keep Pathlib Semantics connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pathlib Semantics model · Theory Pathlib Semantics, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Pathlib Semantics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pathlib Semantics model · Theory Pathlib Semantics, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5594 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a bounded content manifest with explicit rejection, root, and collision evidence; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pathlib Semantics model · Theory Pathlib Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Calling resolve is harmless normalization with no filesystem or trust consequences.
